headline: Baidu debuts AI 'Lobsters' as Nvidia CEO hails OpenClaw
summary: STORY: Lobsters were everywhere at an event hosted by China's leading internet search provider, Baidu, in Beijing this week. It introduced a family of "lobsters," its nickname for AI agents built on OpenClaw, an open source tool.Reuters Correspondent Stephen Nellis covers the technology industry and attended Nvidia’s recent conference where OpenClaw was a hot topic."One way to think about this thing called OpenClaw is a sort of middle management layer for AI employees. So in the AI world, there are these things called agents that act like employees that can go out and use a piece of software for you or carry out a task."(FLASH)"So OpenClaw acts like a bit of a middle manager where you tell the computer what you want done, and then it goes and finds the right employees, the right agents to go carry out that task. It collects all that information and then brings it back to you in language that humans can understand."Think trip planning or pulling together a project.Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang called the technology “the next ChatGPT” and (quote) "the most popular open source project in the history of humanity.”"OpenClaw has open-sourced essentially the operating system of agentic computers. It is no different than how Windows made it possible for us to create personal computers. Now, OpenClaw has made it possible for us to create personal agents."Growing enthusiasm over the technology sent Chinese tech shares up by as much as 22% in recent weeks as companies rolled out a suite of products based on the agent.“this is stunningly exciting”Former Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger sees tremendous potential for the concept but a lot of risks too."OpenClaw's, you know, a couple months old, right? We're barely in this view of how agents are going to get deployed and operationalized. And of course, businesses are lookin g at this a couple of months old. And there's tons of security issues, right. There's a lot of questions of, how do I get access to my data? How do I make it available to my enterprise systems? How secure is it when I've done that?”Companies like Nvidia are already working on this. It is targeting the autonomous AI agent market through NemoClaw, an add‑on system that integrates with OpenClaw to provide privacy and safety controls for AI agents capable of executing a wide range of tasks with minimal human oversight.
